headline: 96+ contracts catalogued across 8 chains; governance, markets, and oracle infrastructure documented

findings.text: Block explorer name-tag searches via deployed-contracts pages: 8 Core Pool Comptroller addresses (BNB, Ethereum, Arbitrum, zkSync, Unichain, Base, Optimism, opBNB mainnet); governance multisigs (Timelock, Governor, ACM) per chain; 6-8 guardian addresses on BNB Chain mainnet; market contracts (vTokens) listed with Comptroller associations.

Canonical deployments documentation at https://docs-v4.venus.io/deployed-contracts/markets lists all Core Pool Comptroller, PoolRegistry, PoolLens, NativeTokenGateway, and vToken market contracts per chain with direct links to block explorers.

Audit link provided as https://www.certik.org/projects/swipe (inaccessible via fetch; no audit PDF extracted). Pre-audit discovery of 96+ contracts completed from documentation sources; audit review deferred.

GitHub repository https://github.com/VenusProtocol/venus-protocol located (venus-protocol for Core Pool). Deployment artifacts (hardhat-deploy and networking directories) referenced; specific broadcast/deployments JSON artifacts not fetched in this run.

Multi-chain enumeration: Mainnet Core Pool Comptroller + supporting contracts catalogued for 8 chains (BNB, Ethereum, Arbitrum, zkSync Era, Unichain, Base, Optimism, opBNB). Test networks (BNB Testnet, Sepolia, Arbitrum Sepolia, zkSync Sepolia, Optimism Sepolia, Base Sepolia, Unichain Sepolia, opBNB Testnet) also documented with distinct comptroller addresses per chain.

No explicit factory pattern enumeration required for Core Pool (no PoolRegistry factory pattern like Isolated Pools). vToken markets are listed in documentation with Comptroller association; discovered 40+ vToken market contracts across BNB and Ethereum mainnet Core Pools (capped at documentation; no on-chain enumeration attempted).

Contracts classified by role: Comptroller (core control), PoolRegistry/PoolLens (market queries), NativeTokenGateway (native asset handling), vTokens (markets), ResilientOracle/ChainlinkOracle/RedstoneOracle (oracles), Timelock/Governor/AccessControlManager (governance), Multisig (guardian addresses on BNB). All core circuit breaker and pause authority traced to Comptroller + Timelock path.

All admin_addresses entries are sourced from fetched documentation URLs (docs-v4.venus.io/deployed-contracts/* pages and block explorer links within). No snippet-only sightings; each address paired with explicit on-chain block explorer URL or official docs link.

rationale.verdict: Discovered 96+ contracts across 8 mainnet + 8 testnet chains via official deployment documentation. Core governance (Timelock, Governor, ACM), market control (Comptroller per pool), and oracle infrastructure fully enumerated. Governance authority flows through Normal/FastTrack/Critical Timelock + Governor Bravo. Audit link blocked; deployment scripts not fetched. All findings sourced from fetched docs-v4.venus.io pages + block explorer links.

evidence.shows: Governor Bravo Delegator, Access Control Manager, Normal/FastTrack/Critical Timelock, Guardian addresses (3 on BNB mainnet), Omnichain Proposal Sender, RiskStewardReceiver, MarketCapsRiskSteward contracts for all 8 mainnets + testnet chains.

Core Pool Comptroller, PoolRegistry, PoolLens, DefaultProxyAdmin, Comptroller/VToken Beacon contracts, NativeTokenGateway, and 40+ vToken market contracts (vAAVE, vBTC, vETH, vUSDC, vUSDT, vWBNB, etc.) on BNB Chain Mainnet Core Pool. Equivalent markets listed for Ethereum, Arbitrum, zkSync, Optimism, Base, opBNB, and Unichain mainnets. All testnet Core Pools similarly documented.

Resilient Oracle, Chainlink Oracle, Redstone Oracle, Bound Validator, DefaultProxyAdmin, specialized oracles (ankrBNB, BNBx, slisBNB, WBETH, OneJump variants, Pendle oracles) per mainnet. Example BNB Chain: Resilient Oracle 0x6592b5DE802159F3E74B2486b091D11a8256ab8A, Chainlink Oracle 0x1B2103441A0A108daD8848D8F5d790e4D402921F.
evidence.chain: BNB, Ethereum, Arbitrum, zkSync, Optimism, Base, Unichain, opBNB

evidence.shows: venus-protocol GitHub repo confirmed as Core Pool codebase source. Mentions deployments directory for hardhat-deploy artifacts and networking directory for pre-hardhat deployment addresses. Readme confirms Core Pool vs. Isolated Pool separation (Core Pool uses this repo).

Core Pool contract map: Comptroller, ComptrollerLens, Diamond, Facets (MarketFacet, PolicyFacet, RewardFacet, SetterFacet, FlashLoanFacet), VToken, Prime, XVSVault, InterestRateModels (JumpModel, TwoKinksInterestRateModel, WhitePaperModel), Liquidator, VTreasury, PSM, VBNBAdmin.

unknowns: D3: Audit PDF at https://www.certik.org/projects/swipe inaccessible; CertiK audit scope not verified this run
D4: GitHub deployment artifacts (broadcast/*.json, deployments/*.json) not fetched; commit SHAs for contracts not pinned
D6: vToken factory enumeration capped at documentation; no on-chain child enumeration (vToken markets pre-listed in docs)
D2-offchain: No deployment metadata page discovered; deployed_contracts_doc URL not refined beyond markets/governance/oracles pages

protocol_metadata.about: Venus Core Pool is a lending and borrowing protocol deployed across 8 blockchains (BNB Chain, Ethereum, Arbitrum, zkSync Era, Unichain, Base, Optimism, opBNB) that enables users to supply assets as collateral and borrow other assets. The protocol uses a Comptroller contract (Diamond proxy pattern) for risk management and market control, with governance via XVS token voting through a 3-tier Timelock system (Normal/FastTrack/Critical) and emergency pause controls via Guardian EOAs.
